checks.basis: NTC at 50 C: R(50C) = R25*exp(B*(1/323.15 - 1/298.15)) = 10k*exp(3977*(...)) ~ 3.563 kOhm (Beta model). Tap = 5V * 10k/(3.563k + 10k) = 3.686V. Vishay R-T table gives ~3.60 kOhm -> ~3.676V, so window +/-2% [3.61, 3.76] absorbs the documented Beta-vs-datasheet divergence away from 25 C. The tap rose from 2.500V (25 C) to ~3.69V (50 C): NTC negative-temperature-coefficient behavior.
